ضيف هي الاسئلة بدون حذف اي سؤال تحت اسم calincal 

---

### **Chapter 1: Introduction to the Human Body**

**1. The sum of all chemical processes that occur in the human body is referred to as:**
A. Metabolism
B. Anabolism
C. Catabolism
D. Homeostasis

**2. Which of the following is the correct level of structural organization from smallest to largest?**
A. Chemical, Cellular, Tissue, Organ, System, Organismal
B. Cellular, Chemical, Tissue, Organ, System, Organismal
C. Tissue, Cellular, Chemical, Organ, System, Organismal
D. Chemical, Tissue, Cellular, System, Organ, Organismal

**3. In the anatomical position, the palms of the hands face:**
A. Posteriorly
B. Anteriorly
C. Medially
D. Laterally

**4. The plane that divides the body into right and left sides is the:**
A. Transverse plane
B. Frontal plane
C. Sagittal plane
D. Oblique plane

**5. The heart is located in which body cavity?**
A. Cranial cavity
B. Vertebral cavity
C. Pericardial cavity
D. Pleural cavity

**6. Which directional term means "closer to the point of attachment of a limb"?**
A. Distal
B. Proximal
C. Lateral
D. Superficial

**7. A condition in which the body's internal environment remains within certain physiological limits is:**
A. Hematopoiesis
B. Homeostasis
C. Hemostasis
D. Hydrolysis

**8. Which organ system produces blood cells and stores minerals?**
A. Integumentary system
B. Muscular system
C. Skeletal system
D. Nervous system

**9. The stomach is primarily located in which abdominal quadrant?**
A. Right Upper Quadrant (RUQ)
B. Left Upper Quadrant (LUQ)
C. Right Lower Quadrant (RLQ)
D. Left Lower Quadrant (LLQ)

**10. The serous membrane that covers the lungs is called the:**
A. Pericardium
B. Peritoneum
C. Pleura
D. Meninges

> **Answers Ch.1:** 1.A | 2.A | 3.B | 4.C | 5.C | 6.B | 7.B | 8.C | 9.B | 10.C

---

### **Chapter 3: The Cellular Level of Organization**

**11. The lipid bilayer of the plasma membrane is permeable to:**
A. Ions
B. Glucose
C. Nonpolar, uncharged molecules
D. Large proteins

**12. Which organelle is the site of protein synthesis?**
A. Smooth ER
B. Ribosome
C. Lysosome
D. Peroxisome

**13. The movement of solutes from an area of high concentration to low concentration without energy is:**
A. Active transport
B. Simple diffusion
C. Phagocytosis
D. Pinocytosis

**14. A red blood cell placed in a hypotonic solution will:**
A. Crenate (shrink)
B. Stay the same
C. Hemolyze (burst)
D. Divide

**15. Which organelle modifies, sorts, and packages proteins?**
A. Golgi complex

B. Mitochondria
C. Nucleus
D. Proteasome

**16. The phase of mitosis where chromosomes line up at the center of the cell is:**
A. Prophase
B. Metaphase
C. Anaphase
D. Telophase

**17. DNA replication occurs during which phase of the cell cycle?**
A. G1 phase
B. S phase
C. G2 phase
D. M phase

**18. The process of transcription produces:**
A. DNA from RNA
B. Protein from mRNA
C. mRNA from DNA
D. tRNA from amino acids

**19. Lysosomes contain:**
A. Oxidative enzymes
B. Digestive enzymes
C. DNA
D. Ribosomes

**20. The cytoskeleton component that helps in cell division (forming the spindle) is:**
A. Microfilaments
B. Intermediate filaments
C. Microtubules
D. Microvilli

> **Answers Ch.3:** 11.C | 12.B | 13.B | 14.C | 15.A | 16.B | 17.B | 18.C | 19.B | 20.C

---

### **Chapter 4: The Tissue Level of Organization**

**21. Which tissue type covers body surfaces and lines hollow organs?**
A. Connective tissue
B. Muscular tissue
C. Epithelial tissue
D. Nervous tissue

**22. Which type of epithelium is best adapted for rapid diffusion (e.g., in lungs)?**
A. Simple squamous
B. Stratified squamous
C. Simple columnar
D. Transitional

**23. Which of the following is NOT a type of connective tissue?**
A. Blood
B. Bone
C. Cartilage
D. Skeletal muscle

**24. The extracellular matrix of connective tissue consists of:**
A. Cells and fibers
B. Protein fibers and ground substance
C. Calcified crystals only
D. Plasma membrane and cytoplasm

**25. Which type of gland secretes its product directly into the bloodstream?**
A. Exocrine gland
B. Endocrine gland
C. Holocrine gland
D. Sudoriferous gland

**26. Intervertebral discs are composed of which strong cartilage?**
A. Hyaline cartilage
B. Elastic cartilage
C. Fibrocartilage
D. Reticular cartilage

**27. Goblet cells are unicellular glands that secrete:**
A. Sweat
B. Oil
C. Mucus
D. Saliva

**28. Which tissue has the poorest capacity for repair/regeneration?**
A. Epithelial tissue
B. Bone tissue
C. Nervous tissue
D. Smooth muscle tissue

**29. Stratified squamous epithelium is found in the:**
A. Lining of the stomach
B. Epidermis of the skin
C. Urinary bladder
D. Air sacs of lungs

**30. The connective tissue fiber that provides great tensile strength is:**
A. Collagen fiber
B. Elastic fiber
C. Reticular fiber
D. Muscle fiber

> **Answers Ch.4:** 21.C | 22.A | 23.D | 24.B | 25.B | 26.C | 27.C | 28.C | 29.B | 30.A

---

### **Chapter 6: Bone Tissue**

**31. The shaft of a long bone is called the:**
A. Epiphysis
B. Metaphysis
C. Diaphysis
D. Periosteum

**32. Which cells are responsible for building new bone matrix?**
A. Osteoclasts
B. Osteoblasts
C. Osteocytes
D. Chondrocytes

**33. The structural unit of compact bone is the:**
A. Trabecula
B. Osteon
C. Lacuna
D. Lamella

**34. Spongy bone is composed of a latticework of thin columns called:**
A. Osteons
B. Canaliculi
C. Trabeculae
D. Perforating canals

**35. Endochondral ossification begins with a model made of:**
A. Fibrous connective tissue
B. Hyaline cartilage
C. Elastic cartilage
D. Compact bone

**36. Which hormone increases blood calcium levels by stimulating osteoclasts?**
A. Calcitonin
B. Parathyroid hormone (PTH)
C. Growth hormone
D. Insulin

**37. The thin membrane lining the medullary cavity is the:**
A. Periosteum
B. Endosteum
C. Perichondrium
D. Epiphyseal plate

**38. Growth in bone length occurs at the:**
A. Articular cartilage
B. Periosteum
C. Epiphyseal plate
D. Diaphysis

**39. A fracture where the bone breaks through the skin is called:**
A. Closed (Simple)
B. Open (Compound)
C. Greenstick
D. Comminuted

**40. Osteocytes are located in small spaces called:**
A. Canaliculi
B. Lacunae
C. Central canals
D. Perforating canals

> **Answers Ch.6:** 31.C | 32.B | 33.B | 34.C | 35.B | 36.B | 37.B | 38.C | 39.B | 40.B

---

### **Chapter 7: The Axial Skeleton**

**41. Which bone forms the forehead?**
A. Parietal
B. Frontal
C. Occipital
D. Sphenoid

**42. The "sella turcica" is a depression in which bone that holds the pituitary gland?**
A. Ethmoid
B. Sphenoid
C. Temporal
D. Frontal

**43. Which vertebra has no body and articulates with the occipital condyles?**
A. Axis (C2)
B. Atlas (C1)
C. Vertebra Prominens (C7)
D. T1

**44. How many pairs of true ribs are there?**
A. 5
B. 7
C. 10
D. 12

**45. The lower jaw bone is the:**
A. Maxilla
B. Zygomatic
C. Mandible
D. Palatine

**46. Which suture joins the parietal bones to the occipital bone?**
A. Coronal
B. Sagittal
C. Lambdoid
D. Squamous

**47. The sternum consists of the manubrium, body, and:**
A. Xiphoid process
B. Styloid process
C. Mastoid process
D. Acromion process

**48. Which abnormal curve is an exaggerated thoracic curvature (Hunchback)?**
A. Lordosis
B. Scoliosis
C. Kyphosis
D. Stenosis

**49. The cribriform plate is part of which bone?**
A. Sphenoid
B. Ethmoid
C. Vomer
D. Nasal

**50. Which bone does NOT articulate with any other bone?**
A. Vomer
B. Hyoid
C. Stapes
D. Coccyx

> **Answers Ch.7:** 41.B | 42.B | 43.B | 44.B | 45.C | 46.C | 47.A | 48.C | 49.B | 50.B

---

### **Chapter 8: The Appendicular Skeleton**

**51. The pectoral girdle consists of the:**
A. Scapula and Humerus
B. Clavicle and Scapula
C. Clavicle and Sternum
D. Scapula and Ribs

**52. The glenoid cavity of the scapula articulates with the:**
A. Head of the humerus
B. Head of the femur
C. Clavicle
D. Sternum

**53. Which bone is the medial bone of the forearm?**
A. Radius
B. Ulna
C. Humerus
D. Tibia

**54. How many carpal bones are there in one wrist?**
A. 5
B. 7
C. 8
D. 14

**55. The "sit down" bone of the pelvis is the:**
A. Ilium
B. Ischium
C. Pubis
D. Sacrum

**56. The longest and heaviest bone in the body is the:**
A. Humerus
B. Tibia
C. Fibula
D. Femur

**57. The lateral malleolus is a feature of the:**
A. Tibia
B. Fibula
C. Femur
D. Talus

**58. Which tarsal bone forms the heel?**
A. Talus
B. Calcaneus
C. Navicular
D. Cuboid

**59. The acetabulum is the socket for the:**
A. Shoulder joint
B. Hip joint
C. Knee joint
D. Elbow joint

**60. The thumb is also known as the:**
A. Hallux
B. Pollex
C. Carpus
D. Tarsus

> **Answers Ch.8:** 51.B | 52.A | 53.B | 54.C | 55.B | 56.D | 57.B | 58.B | 59.B | 60.B

---

### **Chapter 9: Joints**

**61. A slightly movable joint is classified as a(n):**
A. Synarthrosis
B. Amphiarthrosis
C. Diarthrosis
D. Synovial

**62. A fluid-filled sac that reduces friction near a joint is a:**
A. Meniscus
B. Bursa
C. Ligament
D. Tendon

**63. Which movement decreases the angle between two bones?**
A. Extension
B. Flexion
C. Abduction
D. Rotation

**64. Moving a limb away from the midline is called:**
A. Adduction
B. Abduction
C. Pronation
D. Supination

**65. The elbow joint (Humeroulnar) is an example of a:**
A. Pivot joint
B. Hinge joint
C. Ball-and-socket joint
D. Plane joint

**66. Which ligament prevents anterior displacement of the tibia on the femur?**
A. Posterior cruciate ligament (PCL)
B. Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL)
C. Tibial collateral ligament
D. Patellar ligament

**67. Turning the palm anteriorly (upward) is called:**
A. Pronation
B. Supination
C. Inversion
D. Eversion

**68. The shoudler joint is a:**
A. Ball-and-socket joint
B. Hinge joint
C. Saddle joint
D. Pivot joint

**69. A dense connective tissue connecting bone to bone is a:**
A. Tendon
B. Ligament
C. Cartilage
D. Muscle

**70. What type of joint is the suture between skull bones?**
A. Fibrous joint
B. Cartilaginous joint
C. Synovial joint
D. Diarthrosis

> **Answers Ch.9:** 61.B | 62.B | 63.B | 64.B | 65.B | 66.B | 67.B | 68.A | 69.B | 70.A

---

### **Chapter 10: Muscle Tissue**

**71. The property of muscle to return to its original shape after stretching is:**
A. Extensibility
B. Elasticity
C. Contractility
D. Excitability

**72. The plasma membrane of a muscle fiber is called the:**
A. Sarcoplasm
B. Sarcolemma
C. Sarcoplasmic reticulum
D. Myofibril

**73. Calcium ions needed for contraction are stored in the:**
A. T-tubules
B. Sarcoplasmic reticulum
C. Mitochondria
D. Nucleus

**74. The thick myofilaments are composed of the protein:**
A. Actin
B. Myosin
C. Troponin
D. Tropomyosin

**75. The neurotransmitter released at the neuromuscular junction is:**
A. Adrenaline
B. Acetylcholine (ACh)
C. Dopamine
D. Serotonin

**76. The regulatory protein that binds calcium is:**
A. Tropomyosin
B. Troponin
C. Actin
D. Titin

**77. An isometric contraction involves:**
A. Shortening of the muscle
B. Lengthening of the muscle
C. Tension generation without length change
D. Movement of a load

**78. Which energy source provides ATP for the first 15 seconds of activity?**
A. Aerobic respiration
B. Anaerobic glycolysis
C. Creatine phosphate
D. Fatty acids

**79. The pigment in muscle that stores oxygen is:**
A. Hemoglobin
B. Myoglobin
C. Melanin
D. Keratin

**80. A motor unit consists of:**
A. One neuron and all the muscle fibers it stimulates
B. One muscle fiber and all the neurons that stimulate it
C. All the muscles in a group
D. The brain and spinal cord

> **Answers Ch.10:** 71.B | 72.B | 73.B | 74.B | 75.B | 76.B | 77.C | 78.C | 79.B | 80.A

---

### **Chapter 11: The Muscular System**

**81. The muscle attachment to the stationary bone is the:**
A. Insertion
B. Origin
C. Belly
D. Tendon

**82. The muscle that is the primary mover for an action is the:**
A. Antagonist
B. Agonist (Prime mover)
C. Synergist
D. Fixator

**83. Which muscle is responsible for smiling (raising corners of mouth)?**
A. Orbicularis oris
B. Zygomaticus major
C. Platysma
D. Masseter

**84. The primary muscle of breathing is the:**
A. Internal intercostal
B. Diaphragm
C. Rectus abdominis
D. Scalenes

**85. Which muscle extends the forearm at the elbow?**
A. Biceps brachii
B. Brachialis
C. Triceps brachii
D. Deltoid

**86. The "six-pack" muscle of the abdomen is the:**
A. External oblique
B. Rectus abdominis
C. Transversus abdominis
D. Internal oblique

**87. Which of the following is NOT part of the Quadriceps Femoris?**
A. Rectus femoris
B. Vastus lateralis
C. Biceps femoris
D. Vastus medialis

**88. The calf muscle that inserts via the calcaneal (Achilles) tendon is the:**
A. Tibialis anterior
B. Gastrocnemius
C. Sartorius
D. Gracilis

**89. Which muscle abducts the arm at the shoulder?**
A. Pectoralis major
B. Latissimus dorsi
C. Deltoid
D. Teres major

**90. The longest muscle in the body, crossing the hip and knee, is the:**
A. Sartorius
B. Rectus femoris
C. Adductor longus
D. Semitendinosus

> **Answers Ch.11:** 81.B | 82.B | 83.B | 84.B | 85.C | 86.B | 87.C | 88.B | 89.C | 90.A clncail
